purpose: Record the larger neighbor carrier boundary and its complete-graph saturation caveat.
boundary: This does not close Gap A.
This does not prove full G900.
This records a larger 120-record neighbor-carrier signal.
The combined carrier saturates to a complete graph, so it is not a sharp generator.
No independent witness layer is proven.

summary.terminal_reason: The larger 120-record neighbor carrier supports global exposure and stable settling, but the combined relation saturates to a complete graph. This supports neighbor accounting, not a sharp source-native generator.

boundary_record.short_form: Larger neighbor carrier supported, but combined relation saturates.
keeper_lines: The overdrive comes from the neighbors.
Neighbor exposure reaches the whole 120-record background.
Settling still preserves only the four admitted traces.
The larger neighbor carrier supports global exposure and stable settling.
The combined carrier is complete and therefore too saturated to be a sharp generator law.
This supports neighbor accounting, not source-native closure.
positive_results: The admitted traces lie in a connected larger carrier.
The combined carrier reaches all 120 records from the admitted residue.
Every tested shell settles back to the admitted four.
negative_results: The combined carrier is complete with 7140 edges for 120 nodes.
A complete combined graph is too dense to serve as a discriminating generator.
No full 900-state return cycle was found.
No independent witness layer was found.
No source-native WXYZTI generator was derived.
blocked_promotions: Do not call this a sharp source-native generator.
Do not call this full G900.
Do not call Gap A closed.
Do not treat complete-graph reachability as explanatory by itself.
allowed_next_work: Prune the neighbor relation family to find a sharper carrier law.
Search relation subsets that still reach all records but are not complete.
Search for an independent witness layer over the settled admitted residue.
Return to source-native WXYZTI generator derivation using neighbor-accounting as a constraint.

interpretation.main_result: The larger neighbor carrier is supported as a global-exposure carrier, but not as a sharp generator.
interpretation.why_this_matters: The neighbor-accounting mechanism scales from local shell exposure to the full 120-record background.
interpretation.why_not_closure: The combined relation saturates to a complete graph, and no full 900-state return cycle or independent witness layer has been found.
interpretation.next_move: Search for a sharper sparse neighbor-carrier law by pruning relation families.

claim.short_form: Larger neighbor carrier supported, but combined relation saturates.
claim.not_closed_reason: The combined relation saturates to a complete graph, and no full 900-state return cycle or independent witness layer has been found.
claim.next_problem: Search for a sharper sparse neighbor-carrier law by pruning relation families.
